---
title: "Effects of a two-week BMAA practice on emotion regulation among university students"
author: "Hsu, W.-C."
image: "../../assets/study-covers-en/2020-hsu-emotion-regulation.png"
lang: en
language: ../../assets/language-en.yml
toc-title: "On this page"
---

```{r}
#| include: false
source("../../R/bmaa_plot_theme.R")
```

::: {.study-cover}
<img src="../../assets/study-covers-en/2020-hsu-emotion-regulation.png" alt="Depression decreased">

Cover figure: after two weeks of BMAA-S, depression scores decreased in the practice group.
:::

## Method

A pretest-posttest waitlist-control study with university and graduate students. The experimental group received a two-week, nine-hour short-form BMAA practice.

## Key Findings

After two weeks of BMAA-S, students showed lower depression and emotion-regulation difficulty, along with stronger body awareness. Lower depression scores mean fewer depressive symptoms; lower emotion-regulation difficulty means emotions are easier to manage; higher body awareness means participants are better able to notice internal bodily states.

The BMAA-S group moved in a positive direction on all three indicators: depression and emotion-regulation difficulty decreased, while body awareness increased.

```{r}
#| fig-height: 4.8
dat <- data.frame(
  outcome = rep(c("Depression", "Emotion-regulation difficulty", "Body awareness"), each = 4),
  group = rep(c("BMAA-S", "BMAA-S", "Control", "Control"), 3),
  time = rep(c("Pretest", "Posttest", "Pretest", "Posttest"), 3),
  value = c(16.54, 7.36, 15.04, 13.43, 53.39, 42.46, 54.86, 55.46, 78.77, 100.39, 81.04, 83.34)
)
bmaa_facet_line_plot(dat, "Short-form BMAA-S and emotion regulation", ylab = "Mean score")
```

## Reference

許惟智. (2020). *探討兩週身心中軸覺察練習對大學生情緒調節的成效及覺察傾向、工作記憶的中介角色* [碩士論文，國立臺灣大學]. https://doi.org/10.6342/NTU202002076
